_meta.description: Brick-and-mortar gambling regulation classification per US state. Operational-reality lens: states with operating gray-market poker rooms classified as 'limited' even where strict legality is questionable.

categories.limited: Cardrooms / racinos / restricted gaming / gray-market venues operating
categories.prohibited: No operating casino or poker venues

_meta.notes: Online poker / online gambling explicitly OUT OF SCOPE — this layer reflects brick-and-mortar facility regulation only. Day-cruise / offshore gambling boats not represented (no land-based legal status).

Alabama.notes: Poarch Band of Creek Indians operates Wind Creek Atmore/Wetumpka/Montgomery. No commercial casinos. Electronic bingo halls (Greenetrack etc.) operate in legal gray zone.

Alaska.notes: Charity bingo and pull-tabs only; no casino-style gaming.

Arizona.notes: Tribal-only via state compacts (~24 tribes). No commercial casinos.

Arkansas.notes: Commercial casinos legalized 2018 — Oaklawn, Saracen Resort, Southland; plus parimutuel racing.

California.notes: ~78 licensed cardrooms (CGCC) + tribal compacts. No commercial casinos. Cardrooms cannot bank house games.

Colorado.notes: Commercial gaming geographically restricted to Black Hawk, Central City, and Cripple Creek. Plus tribal (Ute Mountain, Sky Ute).

Connecticut.notes: Tribal-only — Foxwoods (Mashantucket Pequot) and Mohegan Sun (Mohegan Tribe). No commercial casinos.

Delaware.notes: Three racinos only — Delaware Park, Dover Downs (Bally's Dover), Harrington Raceway. No standalone commercial casinos.

Florida.notes: Tribal (Seminole/Hard Rock) + parimutuel cardrooms + jai-alai. No commercial standalone casinos. Sports betting via Seminole compact.

Georgia.notes: No legal commercial gaming, but charity poker and gray-market rooms operate. State-line rooms exist near borders.

Hawaii.notes: Strict prohibition — one of two states with no legal gambling at all (no lottery, no charity gaming).

Idaho.notes: Tribal-only — Coeur d'Alene, Shoshone-Bannock, Kootenai. No commercial casinos.

Illinois.notes: Commercial casinos (riverboats + land-based since 2019 expansion) + video gaming terminals + racinos.

Indiana.notes: Riverboats and land-based commercial casinos + two racinos + tribal (Pokagon Four Winds South Bend).

Iowa.notes: Commercial riverboats and land-based casinos; tribal also present (Meskwaki, Winnebago).

Kansas.notes: State-owned commercial casinos (Kansas Star, Boot Hill, Hollywood Kansas Speedway, Kansas Crossing) + tribal.

Kentucky.notes: Historical Horse Racing machines at racetracks (Churchill Downs, Keeneland, Red Mile, etc.); no commercial casinos. Charity gaming.

Louisiana.notes: Riverboats + Harrah's New Orleans land-based + racinos + tribal (Coushatta, Tunica-Biloxi, Jena).

Maine.notes: Two commercial casinos — Hollywood Bangor (Penn Entertainment) and Oxford Casino (Churchill Downs).

Maryland.notes: Six commercial casinos — Live!, MGM National Harbor, Horseshoe Baltimore, Hollywood Perryville, Ocean Downs, Rocky Gap.

Massachusetts.notes: Three commercial casinos — Encore Boston Harbor, MGM Springfield, Plainridge Park.

Michigan.notes: Detroit commercial casinos (MGM Grand, MotorCity, Hollywood Greektown) + 22+ tribal venues.

Minnesota.notes: Tribal-dominant (~18 tribal casinos). Two racinos with cardrooms (Canterbury Park, Running Aces) but no commercial casinos.

Mississippi.notes: Gulf Coast and river commercial casinos (~25 total) + Choctaw tribal (Pearl River, Bok Homa).

Missouri.notes: Riverboat/dockside commercial casinos under MGC oversight; ~13 properties.

Montana.notes: Tavern-style gaming statewide (slot machines + small-stakes poker in bars/restaurants); no full commercial casinos.

Nebraska.notes: Commercial casinos legalized via 2020 ballot initiative; operating at racetracks (WarHorse Lincoln, Grand Island, etc.).

Nevada.notes: Most permissive US gaming jurisdiction. NGCB regulates ~450+ nonrestricted licensees plus restricted (tavern) licenses.

New Hampshire.notes: Charitable gaming model — poker rooms operate as charity 'casinos' with stake limits and required charity payouts.

New Jersey.notes: Atlantic City commercial casinos under DGE regulation; ~9 properties.

New Mexico.notes: Tribal-dominant (~24 tribal venues); 5 racinos exist but tribal is the dominant venue type. No commercial casinos.

New York.notes: Commercial casinos (Resorts World Catskills, Tioga Downs, Rivers Schenectady, del Lago, Rivers) + Seneca/Mohawk/Oneida tribal + racinos + downstate licenses pending.

North Carolina.notes: Tribal-only — Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians (Harrah's Cherokee, Cherokee Valley River, Catawba Two Kings).

North Dakota.notes: Tribal + charity card rooms with limited stakes. No commercial casinos.

Ohio.notes: Four commercial casinos (Hollywood Columbus/Toledo, Hard Rock Cincinnati, JACK Cleveland) + 7 racinos.

Oklahoma.notes: Tribal-dominant — largest concentration of tribal casinos in the US (~70+ Class III venues).

Oregon.notes: Tribal (~11) + small social-gaming parlors / 'casinos' that operate under restricted licenses. No commercial standalone casinos.

Pennsylvania.notes: PGCB regulates commercial casinos (Parx, Sands Bethlehem/Wind Creek, Rivers, Mohegan Pocono, Hollywood, Live!, etc.) + racinos.

Puerto Rico.notes: Commercial casinos in tourist hotels under Puerto Rico Tourism Company regulation.

Rhode Island.notes: Two commercial casinos — Bally's Twin River and Bally's Tiverton.

South Carolina.notes: No legal land-based casino gambling; offshore day-cruise gambling boats operate but not represented in this layer.

South Dakota.notes: Deadwood limited-stakes commercial casinos + tribal (~12 tribal venues).

Tennessee.notes: No commercial casinos, no tribal compacts. Lottery and fantasy sports legal but no brick-and-mortar gaming.

Texas.notes: Restrictive state law but extensive gray-market poker rooms operate (member-fee model). Only one tribal casino (Kickapoo Lucky Eagle); Naskila electronic gaming.

Utah.notes: Strict prohibition — one of two states with no legal gambling of any kind.

Virginia.notes: Commercial casinos legalized 2020 — Hard Rock Bristol, Caesars Danville, Rivers Portsmouth, HeadWaters Norfolk pending.

Washington.notes: Tribal (~31 tribal venues) + ~50 licensed 'mini-casinos' / cardrooms with $5/$10 wager limits. No traditional commercial casinos.

West Virginia.notes: Five commercial casinos (Hollywood Charles Town, Mountaineer, Wheeling Island, Mardi Gras, Greenbrier) — historically racinos but operate as full commercial.

Wisconsin.notes: Tribal-only — ~25 tribal casinos under state compacts. No commercial casinos.

Wyoming.notes: Tribal — Wind River, Northern Arapaho. Some racetracks and historic horse racing machines but tribal is dominant.
